steps.title: Follow the therefore claim
steps.content: The text contrasts a common negative view of interruptions with research showing social benefits: greater belonging and job satisfaction. The conclusion should balance the possible costs with those benefits.

steps.title: Keep the conclusion balanced
steps.content: B overstates the finding by recommending frequent interruptions for productivity, C invents a motive for employees, and D contradicts the research by discouraging interruptions. The matching conclusion says interpersonal benefits can offset perceived negative effects. The answer is A.
